column1: # variant_words.tsv — 借音字 (phonetic-loan character) alias overrides.
# Format: variant_spelling<TAB>canonical_spelling
# Each row means "variant_spelling is a common miswriting of canonical_spelling
# that borrows a homophone's sound" — NOT a true 異體字 (orthographic variant
# with the same underlying reading). The canonical_spelling MUST already
# resolve in word_entries (rime-cantonese / ToJyutping / oral_hk) at build
# time; build_dict.py copies its jyutping verbatim and tags the variant with
# source="variant_alias", so the reading never drifts out of sync with the
# canonical word's own dict entry.
# Root cause (see issue investigation, v2.1.0): the variant character carries
# its OWN legitimate reading elsewhere (e.g. 訓 in 訓練/教訓 is genuinely
# fan3 — nothing wrong there). Char-level fallback has no way to know a
# *specific word* is borrowing a different character's sound, so it silently
# returns the variant character's native reading instead. Word-level alias
# entries are the only fix; there is no general character-level rule.
# License: Apache-2.0 (canto-g2p project, hand-curated). Compiled from public
# linguistic knowledge of documented Cantonese 借音字, independently verified
# — NOT extracted from any single restricted-license dictionary (e.g. words.hk).
# Before adding an entry, check for segmentation collisions: the variant
# spelling must not be a prefix of some OTHER real word sharing the same
# first character(s), or the longest-match segmenter will shadow that real
# word. E.g. `個度` (for 嗰度) was tried and rejected — it collides with the
# real word `度數` inside `個度數` ("this reading/number"), turning it into
# `個度`+`數` instead of `個`+`度數`. Check with:
#   uv run python3 -c "... any(w.startswith(prefix) and len(w) > len(prefix) ..."
# over word_entries before committing a new row.
# Skips lines starting with '#' or blank lines.
訓覺
岩岩
果度
緊係
係度
念住
林住
禁掣
㩒掣
令女
亂嗡
晒氣
甘樣
甘多
吾該
吾知
吾好
